scope: Corrective replication of CAM_FRONT internal geometry and RGB target-support response after fixing the HUGSIM box-height sign convention
hard_constraints: corresponding target and occluder states remain invariant across factorial pairs
saved target-occluder planar offsets match the declared four-metre longitudinal and two/zero-metre lateral separations
ego states, actions, plans, timestamps, camera, and base assets remain paired
target and occluder footprints retain more than 0.1 m clearance
the occluder camera-center depth remains smaller than target depth
partial-only overlap is at most two percent of the target projection and is excluded from the nested RGB-support comparison domain
strong projected target-box coverage is greater than partial coverage and partial coverage is positive
paired RGB differences remain stable in the actor-free background control band
monotonic_relations: at every qualified frame partial target RGB support is not smaller than strong target RGB support
across the complete window median partial target RGB support is strictly greater than median strong target RGB support
synthetic_negative_controls: swapping partial and strong support series must produce a monotonic reversal
placing the occluder behind the target must fail camera-depth ordering
insufficient unoccluded target pixels must be reported unavailable rather than passed
the HUGSIM stored-height sign must be inverted when forming vehicle-frame z
independence_notice: Projection, footprint, and RGB-difference metrics are independently recomputed without HUGSIM semantic, depth, NC, TTC, or PDMS, but their calibration, boxes, and pixels remain simulator-produced rather than independent real-world truth; CF-O 002 is also a corrective repeat after the 001 analysis defect was observed.
strongest_allowed_claim: Within scene-0383, CAM_FRONT, this vehicle asset, trajectory, and the two declared occluder placements, saved geometry has the preregistered occlusion order and rendered target-direct RGB support changes in the expected direction.
forbidden_claims: the numerical occlusion fraction is realistic
HUGSIM RGB or sensor output is equivalent to reality
object detection or tracking under occlusion is validated
closed-loop risk or safety is established
HUGSIM is generally credible as an AD test domain
CF-O 002 is an independent scene or independent statistical replication
